input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges` isn't a standard `ContentPage` property — that's likely why it has no effect. Here's what you actually need:

**1. Remove the navigation bar** (if using Shell or NavigationPage):
```xml
Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
<!-- or -->
NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"
```

**2. iOS — disable safe area insets:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False">
```

**3. Android — ensure edge-to-edge rendering:**

.NET 9+ defaults to edge-to-edge on Android, but the framework may still pad content to avoid system bars. In your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);
    WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false);
}
```

**4. Set the Image to fill:**
```xml
<Image Source="photo.jpg" Aspect="AspectFill"
       HorizontalOptions="Fill" VerticalOptions="Fill" />
```

**5. Make status/nav bars transparent** in `Platforms/Android/Resources/values/styles.xml`:
```xml
<item name="android:statusBarColor">@android:color/transparent</item>
<item name="android:navigationBarColor">@android:color/transparent</item>
```

The combination of `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` (iOS) and `SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false)` (Android) is what actually removes the system bar padding. `SafeAreaEdges` doesn't exist on `ContentPage`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about making content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but the image still has padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what they need:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve true edge-to-edge content that goes behind system bars, you need to:

1. Set the page to ignore safe area insets (which they've done with `SafeAreaEdges="None"` - though the actual API might be different)


2. Handle platform-specific settings since iOS and Android have different approaches — iOS uses a platform-specific property to disable safe area, while Android requires managing window insets for edge-to-edge display. Recent versions of .NET MAUI have improved support for this, particularly in .NET 9 and beyond.

Actually, I'm second-guessing whether `SafeAreaEdges` is a real property on `ContentPage` — it doesn't seem to be part of the standard API. The actual iOS approach uses a platform-specific configuration, and I should verify what the correct cross-platform or platform-specific APIs are for handling safe areas in current .NET MAUI.

Setting up iOS-specific safe area handling...

For Android, the situation is different — newer versions default to edge-to-edge rendering, but the framework might still apply padding by default. If they're wrapping the page in a NavigationPage, that navigation bar will also consume space. The real solution likely involves disabling the navigation bar if they don't need it, and then handling the window insets properly on Android to truly go edge-to-edge.
